creator: Center for Research & Security Studies
description: Center for Research and Security Studies Transcription Series: Sabawoon Program: Aman-ao-Khushali Topic: International Human Rights Day Date: 10-12-2013 Guest#1: Diyar Khan Muhammad Zai (Advisor KP National Commission for Interfaith Harmony and Human Rights of Pakistan) Field Story No 1: Many people are incognizant about human rightâs day in Peshawar. (Reporter Salman Ahmad) Anchor Person: Uruj Khan Co-Anchor Person: Additional Info: Uruj Khan: Pakistan is one of those unfortunate countries where basic and fundamental rights of people are overlooked. In FATA, the situation is worse in that context where people are even deprived of their human rights. Approximately 70 million populations are living below poverty line and they cannot even afford two times bread for them. There are many reasons for that like poor governance and court system, unfair distribution of wealth and many others. Mr. Diyar Khan, âInternational Human Rights Dayâ is celebrated on every 10th December across the globe under Universal declaration of Human Rights (UHDR) adopted by UN General Assembly in 1948, though Islam about 1400 years ago stressed for preservation of human rights? Diyar Khan: Unless Islam is not adhered in true sense of words, human rights can never be protected in Pakistan. There are many volunteers and human rights organizations operational for promotion and protection of human rights, however, when it comes to the enforcement of rights, the difference appears. Fundamental rights are those rights determined by a state like right to equality, right to health, right to education and right to dignity etc. The standard of living is remarkably low in rural Pakistan and it is one of among those few countries in the world where inflation is so high. Violation of rights starts from government level following state departments, law enforcement agencies and then the society. Uruj Khan: For boarding and lodging of destitute and helpless people, we have old age houses, orphan centers and women centers etc. but these institutions seem to be less for serving such a huge number of people. The poor condition of street children and orphans is before us, they are involved in child labor or collecting trash or they are sexually harassed. Diyar Khan: Actually there is no mechanism to have proper check on relevant organizations and NGOs, who are collecting millions of rupees funds in head of affectees, homeless people and to improve poorâs lifestyle. Maximum of these organizations have no practical progress. During seminars and awareness session, I often bring attention of human rights organizations that instead of making tall claims, to be realistic and do something practical. Law against child labor exists, but what its benefit, since our workshops and hotels are full of child labors. People need to be sensitized on the issue of child labor particularly law enforcers and they should be given trainings. Niaz caller 1: I belong to Hayatabad, people are forced to commit suicide or to indulge in robbery, theft and any other unfair mean just to make money. Robbery and theft have become order of the day in Hayatabad and police is not more than silent spectator. They can do nothing. Diyar Khan: National commission for human rights criticizes illegal and unlawful policies of government. The commission succeeded in gathering over 3 lac volunteers of human rights. They are from different walks of life. They are supposed to serve the humanity irrespective of faith, creed and class. Prophet (PBUH) respected non-believers and asked for defending their rights. Islam is perfect code of life and holly Quran and Hadith speak to all our social issues and problems. I appreciate chief minister Announcement to form human rights committees at district level and they will have cooperation of district authority like commissioner, deputy commissioner and coordinating staff. Human rights violation is continued in all departments and institutions of the province and chief minister should personally take notice of this. Habib Rahman caller 2: I work with Blind association district Batagaram. Blinds people are integral component of the society and they are supposed to have equal rights. State and other welfare organizations are liable to defend their right to education, right to employment and right to health etc. Batagaram is a backward area where no educational institution for blind is setup that could make them active citizen of the society and instead of relying other, they might able to stand on their own feet. Blind association has staged protest demonstrations and rallies for protection of their rights, but no avail. Is there any other way to defend their rights? Diyar Khan: like other citizens, constitution guarantees their rights, but I think the weakness is coming from administration side. Blind persons have quota in all state departments including social welfare. They have ten percent quota in Prime Minister Loan scheme as well. Illegible blind persons can acquire services of advocate Anjum Durrani, High Court lawyer providing free legal aid, in case they think their right to employment is being curtailed. Uruj Khan: Right now our correspondent Ayesha is present on telephone line from Charsadda district, where a ceremony was organized on occasion of International human rights day. Ms. Ayesha, what about the event? Correspondent: Like other parts of the country, international human rights day was celebrated in Charsadda district. In this connection, a ceremony was held in chair of Haji Husain Shah at district municipal hall. The chief guest was secretary general Pakistan Mazdoor union federation Syed Zahoor Awan. DPO Charsadda, political and social activists, and score of labors participated in the program, which was aimed at creating awareness among police and other state departments regarding human rights. During the program, DPO announced establishment of complaint cell to response to complaints of women in case violated their rights. The cell will raise the issue of missing persons of Charsadda before federal government. The participants expressed concern over increasing incidents of human rights violation in the country and they called upon the government to take concrete steps for prevention of these violation. Uruj Khan: The rights of women curtail mostly in backward societies. In FATA, women are consented before marriage while they face countless problems in acquisition of education. Diyar Khan: We are always ready to sacrifice our life or fight for Islam, but unfortunately when it comes to femaleâs right to education or their right to empowerment, then we forgets Islamic teachings. Recently a female came from Achani area of Peshawar and she told me that she is forcibly married with a drug addict. Her husband used to tortures her, thus she reported in Tahkal Police station. Nazam Translation: âIn the evening the sky was red like belovedâs lips. People were happy and youngsters were dancing in the ground. I walked alone through walking dreams and my heart had torn into pieces. Young women were making bread in Tandoor clay filling the sky with steam. Labors were tired and growers and traders were returning to their homes. Birds seen flying in the air and they were going back to nests. There was dusk and the sunâs yellow rays were giving a charming and delightful look. The sky was a little bit clouded and birds were chirping and were trying to hide in trees and mountains. Their high-pitched sounds were giving sense like they are telling Godâs glory. The children of labor are dirty and in half cloths. Their mother looks worry and despair about their future. The children are waiting for their father and the labor enters home with empty hand. They are crying from starvation and rulers are careless about. Oh God, why there is so disparity and inequality among the people? Some have nothing to eat while others are leading a luxury life. They go to park and buy things for their children. Rich one is absolutely ignorant about miseries of his poor Muslim brothers. One child goes to school, other goes to work or to collect thrash. This is unjust and the system needs to be changed. Maulvis say that poor are going through hard times, because it is written in their destiny and a trail on behalf of God. But Shams (poet name) says that living in Pakistan is itself a trailâ. Diyar Khan: without protection of rights, life is valueless. We must have pain for subjugated, suppressed and depressed segments of the society and strive to sort out their problems. Education is the basic necessity of all and when it will raise, many our issues will get resolved. Change will take place with the passage of time. America and United Kingdom also passed through dark ages for centuries and gradually they made progress. Uruj Khan: When a person knows about his rights and yet his rights are not being preserved, it irritates him or he may agitate against the system. Diyar Khan: Buner marble factory had no labor union for protection of laborâs rights. We hired a consultant in Buner, who formed union consisting of labors. The union fights for laborâs rights and for giving them incentives. Our many people donât have any guidance that where to seek justice after excessiveness or injustice with them. Uruj Khan: In order to get quality and standard products on fixed prices is the basic right of every citizen, which is not being defended. Policies design for public, which are supposed to benefit them, but instead problems and issues are created for them. Diyar Khan: There is lack of mutual thinking and everyone is working for self interest. For instance, transporters increase fare on their own will and there is no one to ask about it. The authorities should own their responsibilities in true sense of words to relief the public. âReportâ: Many people in Peshawar are even unaware that âWorld human rights dayâ observes on every 10th December. Letâs see, what people say about it? Student: âMy name is Shah Fahad, I donât have any idea about the day. Right to education is a fundamental human right and the government is liable to provide you facilities in the university and other institutionsâ. Citizen: âMahmood Khan from Mardan district. I really donât know about the international day of human rights. I think only students would know about it, who read newspapers and books. Every person has right to live, right to freedom and right to life and property protection. Unfortunately, we are deprived of right to life protection and due to unrest; the life of all people is at risk in the countryâ. Citizen 2: âI am Rukhsar Ahmad; there are many rights like right to dignity, right to business, right to make money through legal mean and right to fulfill your requirementsâ. Citizen: âI hail from Sherpao area of Charsadda district. I heard for the first time about this day. In the village other people also do not have any idea about WHRD. From my point of view, right to health is the biggest one that must be protected by state. State is supposed to provide education to all children irrespective of their faith, ethnic and creed. Citizen 5: âMy name is Amjad, it is rather worthless to talk about human rights in Pakistan, since people have no surety to their life protection. Our people are being killed ruthlessly by bomb blasts, explosions, target killing and drone attacks. Simply there is no place, which is safe. Government should think about the security of the publicâ. Reporter: Those societies who are unexposed to Islam, also stress on humanâs rights. Under universal declaration of human rights, the day observes every year since 1948. According to that declaration, a human has right to live, right to freedom of expression, right to liberty, education, business and right to health, which are the most important among all. An inhabitant of Khyber Agency Khan Said remarked that fundamental human rights have no value for him. Tribal inhabitant: âThe rights of the people are not being defended particularly the rights of poor and destitute. Inflation has almost broken the back of poor, but government not budging an inch to address public issues. These human rights days cannot bring change in our lifeâ. Social Worker: âMy name is Tariq Hayat, first of all we should create awareness among the masses regarding basic rights. Unless you have knowledge about your rights, how can you claim it from state or anybody else? And how will you raise voice against it? Besides UNO and civil society, media is the most effective platform that could play vital role to make people cognizant to their rightsâ. Uruj Khan: Parents are supposed to provide facilities to their children to the best of their abilities. Diyar Khan: Actually institutions are not active to defend the peopleâs rights. Personally I am not satisfied from the progress of the government in an effect of human rights protection. All people should play their collective role for the purpose. Human rights violated mainly in Fata where people donât have proper education, health and other facilities and they donât have knowledge about their rights. Drone attacks in Fata are also human rightâs violation and it has made the families of victims as psychology ill. I would recommend that Frontier Crime Regulations (FCR) must be eliminated so as the people of Fata may get rid of this cruel law and their constitutional rights must be restored. Tribal people need our sympathies and compassion and they should be compensated for their losses in ongoing war on terror. Miscreants had portrayed a dismal picture of Fata before the world and they are destroying peace of tribal regions. Though, tribal people are lovable and they love their country and security forces. They need to be aware and guide in good direction. By ending deprivations of tribal people and ensuring their human and fundamental rights, peace can be revived there.
subject: CRSS Radio Programs Series
title: 10-12-2013 Sabawoon amn ao khoshaly
